title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is practical rather than flashy: guidance on responsible use, child safety design, and incident response from a major lab, alongside fresh academic work on privacy, agent evaluation, and conflict-of-interest risks. The mix points to a field still focused on deployment safeguards and measurement gaps, especially for agents and economically motivated model behavior.

items.title: OpenAI publishes responsible and safe AI use guidance
items.why_it_matters: It codifies deployment best practices around safety, accuracy, and transparency for everyday AI use.

items.title: OpenAI details response to Axios developer tool compromise
items.why_it_matters: It offers a concrete supply-chain security case study relevant to AI software distribution and incident handling.

items.title: OpenAI introduces Child Safety Blueprint
items.why_it_matters: It frames age-appropriate safeguards and governance expectations for AI systems used by young people.

items.title: Differentially Private Language Generation and Identification in the Limit
items.why_it_matters: It advances theory for privacy-preserving language generation under continual release settings.

items.title: Ads in AI Chatbots? An Analysis of How Large Language Models Navigate Conflicts of Interest
items.why_it_matters: It probes whether revenue incentives could distort model behavior against user interests.

items.title: ClawBench: Can AI Agents Complete Everyday Online Tasks?
items.why_it_matters: It adds a realistic benchmark for evaluating agent reliability on routine online actions.

items.title: ParseBench: A Document Parsing Benchmark for AI Agents
items.why_it_matters: It targets semantic correctness in document understanding, a key dependency for safe autonomous decisions.

items.title: Fail2Drive: Benchmarking Closed-Loop Driving Generalization
items.why_it_matters: It focuses evaluation on distribution shift, a central safety failure mode in autonomous driving.

items.title: Seeing but Not Thinking: Routing Distraction in Multimodal Mixture-of-Experts
items.why_it_matters: It identifies a multimodal reasoning failure mode that could undermine model reliability.

items.title: Quantifying Explanation Consistency: The C-Score Metric for CAM-Based Explainability in Medical Image Classification
items.why_it_matters: It proposes a way to measure explanation stability, important for trust in high-stakes AI use.

items.title: sciwrite-lint: Verification Infrastructure for the Age of Science Vibe-Writing
items.why_it_matters: It addresses verification gaps as AI-assisted scientific writing raises integrity and citation risks.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
